page: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 8370C processor cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz, the x86_64 architecture CPU includes a cache hierarchy of 48 KB L1D, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and includes 307 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Performance benchmarks place the E8ds_v5 consistently in the average tier across most standard workloads. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests via stress-ng and Geekbench yield average results, as do memory bandwidth tests, which reach 417.11 GB/sec for cached read-only small blocks and 59.34 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The instance demonstrates average throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized 7B LLM inference tasks show weaker relative performance, falling into the bottom 25% tier.

From a resource density perspective, the E8ds_v5 offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated GPU acceleration. The high memory allocation relative to the vCPU count makes it highly suitable for relational database servers, caching layers, and enterprise applications. The inclusion of 307 GB of local storage further supports workloads requiring fast temporary storage, such as data processing pipelines and medium-scale database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 307 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance delivers balanced, mid-tier performance across standard benchmarks, including stress-ng, Geekbench, and Redis operations. While it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and medium LLM inference, its high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU makes it a highly efficient choice for memory-bound applications. The E8ds_v5 is best suited for relational databases, caching systems, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, delivering reliable mid-tier performance for database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, ideal for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for database and caching workloads.

64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory allocation
307 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities
Average performance across CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis benchmarks
Well-suited for relational databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive workloads
